code_paths_examined: repos/noetl/noetl/worker/nats_worker.py:_execute_step result handling and terminal event emission
repos/noetl/noetl/server/api/execution/endpoint.py execution detail and event read paths

potential_risk: Worker _extract_control_context still scalarizes arbitrary nested non-error dictionaries unless they are reference wrappers or the special error.diagnosis shape. Current reviewed contracts are covered, but future nested control contracts need explicit carve-outs and parity tests.

Worker blocked data-plane keys intentionally exclude data, response, result, payload, rows, and columns from strict context. Any future semantic control metadata placed under those keys must be persisted through references or a reviewed context field.

Server _collect_compact_context copies only selected top-level transport fields. It is safe as a compact merge helper, but should not become the only persistence route for future nested control metadata.
safe_by_design: Agent envelopes are preserved by the agent tool return path and the generic error-coercion carve-out.
Agent error.diagnosis is now explicitly preserved in worker event context by noetl/noetl#417.
Task-sequence errors use scalar error.kind and error.message fields, which survive projection.
MCP result content is data-plane output and is carried via response or references rather than result.context.
Playbook data.* is intentionally treated as data-plane output and bounded by result processing/reference preservation.
Externalized payloads store the full original value before event projection emits a reference wrapper.
Server _bounded_context preserves nested dictionaries by byte size rather than scalarizing them.
Execution detail and /executions/{id}/events read paths return persisted context/result JSON without additional projection.
Diagnostic LEFT(result::text, 4000) queries are bounded debug snapshots, not the parity source of truth.

deferred_findings: Potential worker control-context scalarization should get a design pass before expanding nested contracts beyond error.diagnosis.

C.static_smoke_result: OK static v2.35.9-shaped fixture preserves nested diagnosis; OK static v2.35.8-regression fixture detected NESTED_DICT_LOSS at result.context.error.diagnosis; 2/2 live-vs-persisted parity static checks passed
C.cluster_smoke_result: OK cluster parity for execution 620335758527693503: 6 terminal events, 4 comparisons

iterations.summary: Added GHCR manifest probe to bump_image, then added a defensive in-step probe after discovering a failed verify step could still fall through to the rollout loop. Merged noetl/ops#37.
Negative fake tag failed cleanly without mutating noetl-server; positive v2.35.9 idempotent run completed with no image changes.
Audited worker projection, server write path, and execution read path. No new confirmed bug; documented potential nested-control risk and safe-by-design paths.

iterations.action: Phase C static parity smoke and docs PR
iterations.summary: Added scripts/live_vs_persisted_parity_smoke.py, static fixture mode passed, documented static and live usage, merged noetl/docs#27.

iterations.action: Phase C live spike and parity smoke
iterations.summary: Spike execution 620335758527693503 was GREEN. Parity smoke passed against the persisted execution.

notes: All three phases delivered. Phase B did not land a noetl fix because no new confirmed event-projection bug was found; the only deferred item is the documented potential risk that future nested non-error control dictionaries still need explicit projection design. No noetl release happened in this task, so codex-spike-green-validation.md was not updated.
